title: Start Here – IS3513
headerSubtitle: Complete these 4 checkpoints before starting any labs

sections.headline: Welcome to NEXUS Security. You’re the new analyst.
sections.dek: A 15-slide tour of how the course works, who you’ll be working with, and what’s due in your first 72 hours.
sections.embed: <iframe class="nx-video-embed__iframe" src="https://utsa.hosted.panopto.com/Panopto/Pages/Embed.aspx?id=df5f67b7-89de-43c8-b5f6-b455014dce86&autoplay=false&offerviewer=true&showtitle=true&showbrand=true&captions=false&interactivity=all" allowfullscreen allow="autoplay" aria-label="Panopto Embedded Video Player" aria-description="Summer Welcome"></iframe>
sections.caption: I’m John Newsom. This is the Summer 2026 section of Information Assurance and Security. Watch this before you touch a lab — it covers the two questions that drive every assignment in this course, the four Texas clients you’ll work with, how a module is structured, and where to get help.

sections.html: Complete these 4 checkpoints in order before diving into assignments. Each step ensures you’re set up for success.

sections.label: ✓ 1. Read the Syllabus
paragraphs: The syllabus is your contract for grading, policies, and expectations. It covers late work policy, drop policies, academic integrity, and everything else that governs this course. Find it in your Canvas course navigation (usually labeled “Syllabus” in the left sidebar).

sections.label: ✓ 2. Obtain the Textbook
paragraphs: Required: Conklin & White, Principles of Computer Security: CompTIA Security+ and Beyond, Sixth Edition (ISBN: 9781260474312). McGraw-Hill Education.
items: UTSA Bookstore — Physical or digital copies available
Amazon / other retailers — ISBN lookup for used copies; ensure 6th edition

image.alt: Cover of Principles of Computer Security, 6th Edition by Conklin and White

sections.label: ✓ 3. Join Discord (Optional but Recommended)
paragraphs: Discord is the fastest way to get help in this course. Most questions are answered within minutes by classmates or the instructor. You must read the Terms of Service before joining.

sections.label: ✓ 4. Start Lab 1.1
paragraphs: Your Kali Linux VM is your security workstation for the entire semester. Setup happens inside Lab 1.1 — don’t set it up separately. Lab 1.1 walks you through installation, the VM naming convention, and the screenshot rules you’ll need for every assignment going forward. Find Lab 1.1 in your Canvas course modules.

VM naming convention: Name your VM kali-<your abc123> — your abc123 is the ID you use to log into Canvas (for example, aaa000 would be kali-aaa000), not your Banner ID number. This name must be visible in every screenshot. Lab 1.1 explains why.

sections.label: What’s Next?
sections.html: After completing all 4 checkpoints, head to Module 1: Threat Intelligence & Reconnaissance to begin your NEXUS journey.
Questions? Check How to Get Help or post in Discord. Don’t wait until you’re stuck — ask early.
